title: Recipe Cards — Styled with Selectors
description: Organize your recipes into styled cards. Add at least 2 div elements with class="recipe-card", each containing a recipe (h2, description paragraph, etc.). Style .recipe-card with border, padding, margin, and border-radius. Use descendant selectors to style .recipe-card h2 and .recipe-card p differently from the rest of the page.
test_code:
assert 'class="recipe-card"' in html.lower() or "class='recipe-card'" in html.lower(), 'Must include at least one div with class="recipe-card"'
assert html.lower().count('recipe-card') >= 4, 'Must have at least 2 recipe-card divs (class appears in HTML and CSS)'
assert '.recipe-card' in html.lower(), 'Must style .recipe-card in CSS'
assert 'border' in html.lower(), 'Must set border on recipe cards'
assert 'padding' in html.lower(), 'Must set padding on recipe cards'
assert 'border-radius' in html.lower(), 'Must set border-radius on recipe cards'
assert '<nav>' in html.lower(), 'Must keep nav from previous step'
assert '<style>' in html.lower(), 'Must include a <style> block'
acceptance_criteria: At least 2 divs with class="recipe-card" wrap individual recipes
.recipe-card is styled with border, padding, margin, and border-radius
Descendant selectors (.recipe-card h2, .recipe-card p) are used
Navigation menu is preserved from previous step
All previous semantic structure is maintained
hints: Card style: .recipe-card { border: 1px solid #ddd; padding: 20px; margin: 15px 0; border-radius: 8px; }
Add a second recipe card with a different dish to meet the requirement
